description: When aggregator.command resolves to an external adapter (codex-review-prompter), the orchestrator packages the judge-mode system prompt into the adapter Task call's config.judge_mode_prompt field. This fixture records the Task call payload for assertion.

consolidated_findings_ready_for_aggregat.title: Missing idempotency key on payment creation
consolidated_findings_ready_for_aggregat.description: createPayment does not send an idempotency key, risking duplicate charges on network retries.

consolidated_findings_ready_for_aggregat.title: Cart contents not re-validated before payment
consolidated_findings_ready_for_aggregat.description: Prices are read from the client cart without server-side re-validation, enabling price manipulation.

consolidated_findings_ready_for_aggregat.title: N+1 inventory check in checkout loop
consolidated_findings_ready_for_aggregat.description: A separate DB query is issued per line item to verify stock, causing N+1 query behavior for large carts.

consolidated_findings_ready_for_aggregat.title: No transaction rollback on partial payment failure
consolidated_findings_ready_for_aggregat.description: If the payment gateway call succeeds but the order record creation fails, the charge is taken without an order record, leaving the customer in a bad state.

expected_assertions.judge_mode_prompt_exact_text: You are acting as an impartial judge consolidating findings from multiple reviewers. Evaluate each finding on its merits, free of attribution bias. Position randomization has been applied to the input.

notes.external_path: When aggregator.command is a concrete adapter name (codex-review-prompter), orchestrator uses the external-aggregator path (Step 8g)
notes.prompt_location: judge_mode_prompt is injected into config object of the adapter Task call envelope
notes.adapter_responsibility: The adapter (codex-review-prompter) is responsible for surfacing this prompt to the underlying CLI as a system message
notes.raw_string_match: Test asserts exact judge_mode_prompt text matches orchestrator.md documentation
